Defendant Doordash, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss, Or, In The Alternative, Stay The Action Due To Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Sep-28-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances
Mark L. Webb, Esq., Law Office of Mark L. Webb (415) 515-0960, mark@markwebb.com, for Plaintiffs JEFF TAYLOR and DEIDRE TAYLOR. via Zoom Conference
Charles T. Meyer, Esq., STRONGIN|BURGER LLP, (949) 529-2250, cmeyer@stronginburger.com, for Defendant DOORDASH, INC.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT DOORDASH, INC.'S Motion To Dismiss, Or, In The Alternative, Stay The Action Due To Forum Non Conveniens is taken under submission.

After further review, Court will send a tentative ruling to all counsel present. Counsel for the prevailing party will be required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
